description: HP/Agilent E4418B EPM-series single-channel RF power meter, programmable via SCPI over GPIB (and RS-232/RS-422). The shared programming guide also covers the dual-channel E4419B; channel/window selection uses [1|2] suffixes. Supports Agilent E-Series and 8480-series sensors with sensor calibration-factor tables, averaging, limits, math, and recorder/TTL outputs.

identity.description: Returns manufacturer, model (e.g. E4418B or E4419B), serial number and firmware revision as a comma-separated string.

commands.name: configure_power
commands.description: Configures the meter for a single absolute power measurement on the selected channel/window without initiating it. Presets defaults appropriate to the measurement; results are retrieved later with READ? or FETCh?.

commands.name: configure_power_relative
commands.description: Configures the meter for a relative power measurement (measured value referenced to the stored relative value) on the selected channel.

commands.name: configure_power_difference
commands.description: Configures the meter for a difference (channel A minus channel B) power measurement on the selected window.

commands.name: configure_power_difference_relative
commands.description: Configures a relative difference measurement: the A-minus-B difference referenced to the stored relative value.

commands.name: configure_power_ratio
commands.description: Configures the meter for a ratio (channel A / channel B) power measurement on the selected window.

commands.name: configure_power_ratio_relative
commands.description: Configures a relative ratio measurement: the A/B ratio referenced to the stored relative value.

commands.name: fetch_power
commands.description: Returns the most recent absolute power measurement result from the selected channel without re-triggering an acquisition.

commands.name: fetch_power_relative
commands.description: Returns the most recent relative power measurement result from the selected channel.

commands.name: fetch_power_difference
commands.description: Returns the most recent difference (A minus B) power measurement result.

commands.name: fetch_power_difference_relative
commands.description: Returns the most recent relative difference measurement result.

commands.name: fetch_power_ratio
commands.description: Returns the most recent ratio (A / B) power measurement result.

commands.name: fetch_power_ratio_relative
commands.description: Returns the most recent relative ratio measurement result.

commands.name: read_power
commands.description: Triggers a new absolute power acquisition on the selected channel and returns the result (equivalent to ABORt; INITiate; FETCh?).

commands.name: read_power_relative
commands.description: Triggers and returns a relative power measurement on the selected channel.

commands.name: read_power_difference
commands.description: Triggers and returns a difference (A minus B) power measurement.

commands.name: read_power_difference_relative
commands.description: Triggers and returns a relative difference measurement.

commands.name: read_power_ratio
commands.description: Triggers and returns a ratio (A / B) power measurement.

commands.name: read_power_ratio_relative
commands.description: Triggers and returns a relative ratio measurement.

commands.name: measure_power
commands.description: Configures, triggers and returns a single absolute power measurement in one operation (CONFigure + READ?).

commands.name: measure_power_relative
commands.description: Configures, triggers and returns a single relative power measurement.

commands.name: measure_power_difference
commands.description: Configures, triggers and returns a single difference (A minus B) measurement.

commands.name: measure_power_difference_relative
commands.description: Configures, triggers and returns a single relative difference measurement.

commands.name: measure_power_ratio
commands.description: Configures, triggers and returns a single ratio (A / B) measurement.

commands.name: measure_power_ratio_relative
commands.description: Configures, triggers and returns a single relative ratio measurement.

commands.name: calculate_gain_magnitude
commands.description: Sets the display offset (gain/loss) value applied to the measured result for the selected window.

commands.name: calculate_gain_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the display offset (gain) for the selected window.

commands.name: calculate_limit_clear_auto
commands.description: Controls whether the limit fail count and fail flag are cleared automatically. ONCE clears them immediately for the next INITiate.

commands.name: calculate_limit_clear_immediate
commands.description: Immediately clears the limit fail count and the fail flag for the selected window.

commands.name: calculate_limit_fail
commands.description: Queries the limit fail flag for the selected window. Returns 1 if a limit failure has occurred since the count was last cleared, otherwise 0.

commands.name: calculate_limit_fcount
commands.description: Queries the number of limit-failure events (fail count) for the selected window.

commands.name: calculate_limit_lower
commands.description: Sets the lower limit value used for limit checking on the selected window.

commands.name: calculate_limit_state
commands.description: Enables or disables limit checking for the selected window.

commands.name: calculate_limit_upper
commands.description: Sets the upper limit value used for limit checking on the selected window.

commands.name: calculate_math_expression
commands.description: Sets the math expression that defines what the selected window measures (e.g. single channel, ratio or difference of sensor inputs).

commands.name: calculate_math_expression_catalog
commands.description: Returns a comma-separated list of the math expressions available for the selected window.

commands.name: calculate_relative_magnitude_auto
commands.description: Sets the reference value for relative measurements. ONCE captures the current measured value as the relative reference.

commands.name: calculate_relative_state
commands.description: Enables or disables relative-mode measurements for the selected window.

commands.name: calibration_all
commands.description: Performs a complete calibration sequence (zero plus calibration) on the selected channel. The query form returns a pass/fail result.

commands.name: calibration_auto
commands.description: Controls automatic calibration. ONCE performs a calibration immediately on the selected channel.

commands.name: calibration_econtrol_state
commands.description: Enables or disables external power-reference (calibrator) control during calibration on the selected channel.

commands.name: calibration_rcalibration
commands.description: Enables or disables the request-for-calibration-factor prompt for the reference calibration on the selected channel.

commands.name: calibration_rcfactor
commands.description: Sets the reference calibration factor used during calibration on the selected channel.

commands.name: calibration_zero_auto
commands.description: Controls auto-zeroing of the selected channel. ONCE performs a zero immediately; the sensor input must have no applied power.

commands.name: display_contrast
commands.description: Sets the contrast level of the front-panel display.

commands.name: display_enable
commands.description: Enables or disables (blanks) the front-panel display. Disabling can increase measurement throughput.

commands.name: display_window_format
commands.description: Selects the display format for the window: a single numeric measurement or a dual numeric-plus-analog-meter layout.

commands.name: display_window_meter_lower
commands.description: Sets the value represented by the lower (left) end of the analog meter scale for the window.

commands.name: display_window_meter_upper
commands.description: Sets the value represented by the upper (right) end of the analog meter scale for the window.

commands.name: display_window_resolution
commands.description: Sets the displayed measurement resolution (number of significant digits) for the window.

commands.name: display_window_select
commands.description: Selects the specified window as the active (highlighted) window on the display.

commands.name: display_window_state
commands.description: Enables or disables (shows/hides) the specified display window.

commands.name: format_readings_border
commands.description: Sets the byte order for binary (REAL) reading transfers: normal or byte-swapped.

commands.name: format_readings_data
commands.description: Sets the data format used to return measurement readings: ASCII text or REAL (binary) data.

commands.name: memory_catalog_all
commands.description: Returns a catalog of all memory items (instrument states and sensor cal/data tables), with the total bytes used and available.

commands.name: memory_catalog_state
commands.description: Returns a catalog of the stored instrument-state memory items.

commands.name: memory_catalog_table
commands.description: Returns a catalog of the stored sensor calibration/data tables.

commands.name: memory_clear_name
commands.description: Clears (deletes the contents of) the named memory item, freeing its memory.

commands.name: memory_clear_table
commands.description: Clears the contents of the currently selected sensor calibration/data table.

commands.name: memory_free_all
commands.description: Returns the bytes of memory used and available for all memory items.

commands.name: memory_free_state
commands.description: Returns the bytes of memory used and available for instrument-state storage.

commands.name: memory_free_table
commands.description: Returns the bytes of memory used and available for sensor calibration/data tables.

commands.name: memory_nstates
commands.description: Returns the number of instrument-state memory registers available.

commands.name: memory_state_catalog
commands.description: Returns a list of the names assigned to the stored instrument-state registers.

commands.name: memory_state_define
commands.description: Associates a name with an instrument-state register number (or queries the register number for a name).

commands.name: memory_table_frequency
commands.description: Enters the frequency points into the currently selected sensor calibration table (as a comma-separated list).

commands.name: memory_table_frequency_points
commands.description: Returns the number of frequency points in the currently selected sensor calibration table.

commands.name: memory_table_gain
commands.description: Enters the calibration-factor (gain) values into the currently selected sensor calibration table, corresponding to the table's frequency points.

commands.name: memory_table_gain_points
commands.description: Returns the number of calibration-factor (gain) points in the currently selected sensor calibration table.

commands.name: memory_table_move
commands.description: Renames a sensor calibration/data table by moving its contents from an old name to a new name.

commands.name: memory_table_select
commands.description: Selects the sensor calibration/data table that subsequent MEMory:TABLe commands act upon.

commands.name: output_recorder_limit_lower
commands.description: Sets the power value that corresponds to the minimum (0 V) output of the selected rear-panel recorder output.

commands.name: output_recorder_limit_upper
commands.description: Sets the power value that corresponds to the maximum (full-scale) output of the selected rear-panel recorder output.

commands.name: output_recorder_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the selected rear-panel recorder (analog) output.

commands.name: output_roscillator_state
commands.description: Turns the 50 MHz 1 mW POWER REF calibrator output on or off.

commands.name: output_ttl_active
commands.description: Sets the active (asserted) logic level of the selected rear-panel TTL output.

commands.name: output_ttl_feed
commands.description: Selects the internal signal (e.g. a limit-fail summary) that drives the selected rear-panel TTL output.

commands.name: output_ttl_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the selected rear-panel TTL output.

commands.name: sense_average_count
commands.description: Sets the averaging filter length (number of readings averaged) for the selected channel.

commands.name: sense_average_count_auto
commands.description: Enables or disables automatic selection of the averaging filter length for the selected channel. ONCE sets it once based on the current signal.

commands.name: sense_average_sdetect
commands.description: Enables or disables step detection, which shortens the averaging filter response when a large change in power is detected on the selected channel.

commands.name: sense_average_state
commands.description: Enables or disables averaging (the filter) for the selected channel.

commands.name: sense_correction_cset_select
commands.description: Selects the sensor calibration-factor table (CSET) to use for the selected channel.

commands.name: sense_correction_cset_state
commands.description: Enables or disables use of the selected sensor calibration-factor table (CSET) for the selected channel.

commands.name: sense_correction_dcycle_input
commands.description: Sets the duty-cycle value used to correct pulsed-power (DCYCle/GAIN3) measurements on the selected channel.

commands.name: sense_correction_dcycle_state
commands.description: Enables or disables duty-cycle (DCYCle/GAIN3) correction for the selected channel.

commands.name: sense_correction_cfactor_gain_input
commands.description: Sets the calibration-factor (GAIN1) or display-offset gain (GAIN2) input value for the selected channel.

commands.name: sense_correction_gain2_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the display-offset gain (GAIN2) correction for the selected channel.

commands.name: sense_correction_fdoffset_input
commands.description: Queries the frequency-dependent offset (FDOFfset/GAIN4) value currently applied for the selected channel (read-only).

commands.name: sense_correction_loss2_input
commands.description: Sets the external loss/offset (LOSS2) value for the selected channel, used to compensate for cable or attenuator loss.

commands.name: sense_correction_loss2_state
commands.description: Enables or disables the external loss/offset (LOSS2) correction for the selected channel.

commands.name: sense_frequency
commands.description: Sets the input signal frequency for the selected channel so the meter can apply the correct calibration factor (by interpolation from the sensor cal table or sensor EEPROM).

parameters.name: numeric_value
parameters.range: 1 kHz to 999.999 GHz | DEF | MIN | MAX

commands.name: sense_limit_clear_auto
commands.description: Controls whether the channel limit fail count and flag clear automatically. ONCE clears them for the next INITiate.

commands.name: sense_limit_clear_immediate
commands.description: Immediately clears the channel limit fail count and flag.

commands.name: sense_limit_fail
commands.description: Queries the channel limit fail flag. Returns 1 if a limit failure has occurred since the count was cleared, otherwise 0.

commands.name: sense_limit_fcount
commands.description: Queries the channel limit fail count (number of limit-failure events).

commands.name: sense_limit_lower
commands.description: Sets the channel lower limit value used for limit checking.

commands.name: sense_limit_state
commands.description: Enables or disables channel limit checking.

commands.name: sense_limit_upper
commands.description: Sets the channel upper limit value used for limit checking.

commands.name: sense_power_ac_range
commands.description: Sets the measurement range (lower or upper) for the selected channel. Used with E-Series and N8480 (excluding Option CFT) sensors; setting it disables autoranging.

commands.name: sense_power_ac_range_auto
commands.description: Enables or disables autoranging for the selected channel (E-Series and N8480 non-CFT sensors). When ON, the meter selects the best measuring range automatically.

commands.name: sense_speed
commands.description: Sets the measurement speed (readings per second) for the selected channel. 20 and 40 readings/s allow full functionality; 200 readings/s (fast mode) restricts some features.

commands.name: sense_v2p
commands.description: Selects the voltage-to-power conversion (linearity correction) table type used for the connected sensor on the selected channel.

commands.name: status_operation_event
commands.description: Accesses the Operation Status register set: CONDition?, EVENt? (default query), ENABle, PTRansition and NTRansition. Reports overall operation status.

commands.name: status_operation_calibrating
commands.description: Accesses the Operation Calibrating summary register set (CONDition?, EVENt?, ENABle, PTRansition, NTRansition); indicates calibration in progress.

commands.name: status_operation_llfail
commands.description: Accesses the Operation Lower-Limit-Fail summary register set; indicates a lower limit failure.

commands.name: status_operation_measuring
commands.description: Accesses the Operation Measuring summary register set; indicates a measurement is in progress.

commands.name: status_operation_sense
commands.description: Accesses the Operation Sense summary register set; indicates the meter is reading data from the sensor EEPROM.

commands.name: status_operation_trigger
commands.description: Accesses the Operation Trigger summary register set; indicates the meter is waiting for or processing a trigger.

commands.name: status_operation_ulfail
commands.description: Accesses the Operation Upper-Limit-Fail summary register set; indicates an upper limit failure.

commands.name: status_preset
commands.description: Presets the OPERation and QUEStionable status enable and transition registers to their default states.

commands.name: status_questionable
commands.description: Accesses the Questionable Status register set (CONDition?, EVENt?, ENABle, PTRansition, NTRansition); reports questionable measurement conditions.

commands.name: status_questionable_calibration
commands.description: Accesses the Questionable Calibration summary register set; indicates a calibration error condition.

commands.name: status_questionable_power
commands.description: Accesses the Questionable Power summary register set; indicates a questionable power measurement (e.g. over/under range).

commands.name: system_communicate_gpib_address
commands.description: Sets the meter's GPIB (HP-IB) bus address.

commands.name: system_communicate_serial_control_dtr
commands.description: Sets the behaviour of the RS-232 DTR control line.

commands.name: system_communicate_serial_control_rts
commands.description: Sets the behaviour of the RS-232 RTS control line.

commands.name: system_communicate_serial_receive_baud
commands.description: Sets the serial receive baud rate.

commands.name: system_communicate_serial_receive_bits
commands.description: Sets the number of serial receive data bits.

commands.name: system_communicate_serial_receive_pace
commands.description: Sets the serial receive software flow control (handshaking).

commands.name: system_communicate_serial_receive_parity
commands.description: Sets the serial receive parity type.

commands.name: system_communicate_serial_receive_sbits
commands.description: Sets the number of serial receive stop bits.

commands.name: system_communicate_serial_transmit_auto
commands.description: Queries whether the serial transmit settings automatically follow the receive settings.

commands.name: system_communicate_serial_transmit_baud
commands.description: Sets the serial transmit baud rate (when not auto-linked to receive).

commands.name: system_communicate_serial_transmit_bits
commands.description: Sets the number of serial transmit data bits.

commands.name: system_communicate_serial_transmit_echo
commands.description: Enables or disables serial character echo.

commands.name: system_communicate_serial_transmit_pace
commands.description: Sets the serial transmit software flow control (handshaking).

commands.name: system_communicate_serial_transmit_parity
commands.description: Sets the serial transmit parity type.

commands.name: system_communicate_serial_transmit_sbits
commands.description: Sets the number of serial transmit stop bits.

commands.name: system_error
commands.description: Returns the next entry from the instrument's error/event queue as <code>,"<message>".

commands.name: system_language
commands.description: Selects the remote programming language/command set used by the meter.

commands.name: system_local
commands.description: Returns the instrument to local (front-panel) control (RS-232/RS-422 use).

commands.name: system_preset
commands.description: Presets the instrument to a known measurement state (front-panel preset), without affecting GPIB address or stored states.

commands.name: system_remote
commands.description: Places the instrument in remote control mode and locks out the front panel (RS-232/RS-422 use).

commands.name: system_rinterface
commands.description: Selects the active remote interface used to control the meter.

commands.name: system_rwlock
commands.description: Places the instrument in remote-with-lockout state (front panel fully locked, RS-232/RS-422 use).

commands.name: system_version
commands.description: Returns the SCPI version to which the instrument complies (YYYY.V format).

commands.name: abort
commands.description: Aborts any measurement in progress on the selected channel and returns the trigger system to idle.

commands.name: initiate_continuous
commands.description: Sets the selected channel to continuous (free-run) measurement initiation when ON, or single-shot when OFF.

commands.name: initiate_immediate
commands.description: Initiates a single measurement cycle on the selected channel (when not in continuous mode).

commands.name: trigger_delay_auto
commands.description: Enables or disables automatic settling-time delay before a triggered measurement on the selected channel.

commands.name: trigger_immediate
commands.description: Causes an immediate trigger event on the selected channel, regardless of the trigger source setting.

commands.name: trigger_source
commands.description: Selects the trigger source for the selected channel: software BUS (*TRG/GET), IMMediate (free run), or HOLD (no trigger).

commands.name: unit_power
commands.description: Sets the measurement units for the selected window's absolute power readings. Also sets the default power suffix for relevant SENSe commands. Default is dBm.

commands.name: unit_power_ratio
commands.description: Sets the measurement units for the selected window's ratio readings. Default is dB.

commands.name: service_option
commands.description: Sets or queries the instrument option configuration string (service use).

commands.name: service_sensor_cdate
commands.description: Returns the calibration date stored in the connected sensor's EEPROM for the selected channel (E-Series sensors).

commands.name: service_sensor_cplace
commands.description: Returns the calibration place stored in the connected sensor's EEPROM for the selected channel.

commands.name: service_sensor_snumber
commands.description: Returns the serial number stored in the connected sensor's EEPROM for the selected channel.

commands.name: service_sensor_type
commands.description: Returns the sensor type stored in the connected sensor's EEPROM for the selected channel.

commands.name: service_snumber
commands.description: Sets or queries the instrument's serial number (service use).

commands.name: service_version_processor
commands.description: Returns (or sets, service use) the processor firmware version string.

commands.name: service_version_system
commands.description: Returns (or sets, service use) the system firmware version string.

commands.name: clear_status
commands.description: Clears the Standard Event Status register, the error/event queue and other event registers.

commands.name: define_device_trigger
commands.description: Defines the command sequence executed when a Group Execute Trigger (GET) or *TRG is received.

parameters.name: data
parameters.range: arbitrary block program data or string program data

commands.name: event_status_enable
commands.description: Sets the Standard Event Status Enable register mask.

commands.name: event_status_register
commands.description: Reads and clears the Standard Event Status register (returns NR1 0 to 255).

commands.name: identify
commands.description: Returns the instrument identification string: manufacturer, model, serial number and firmware revision.

commands.name: operation_complete
commands.description: Sets the Operation Complete bit in the Standard Event Status register when all pending operations finish; the query (*OPC?) returns 1 when complete.

commands.name: option_identify
commands.description: Returns a comma-separated list of installed instrument options.

commands.name: recall_state
commands.description: Recalls a previously stored instrument state from the specified register.

commands.name: reset
commands.description: Resets the instrument to its default measurement state.

commands.name: save_state
commands.description: Saves the current instrument state to the specified register.

commands.name: service_request_enable
commands.description: Sets the Service Request Enable register mask.

commands.name: status_byte
commands.description: Reads the Status Byte register (returns NR1 0 to 255) without clearing it.

commands.name: trigger
commands.description: Triggers the instrument (equivalent to a Group Execute Trigger) when the trigger source is set to BUS.

commands.name: self_test
commands.description: Initiates the instrument self-test and returns the result (0 = pass, non-zero = failure).

commands.name: wait
commands.description: Prevents the instrument from executing further commands until all pending overlapped operations complete.
